data.playwright_code:
async def get_bins(page, postcode, uprn):
    await page.goto('https://www.broxbourne.gov.uk/bin-collection-date')
    await page.wait_for_timeout(8000)
    try:
        cookie_btn = await page.wait_for_selector("//button[contains(text(), 'Allow all')]", timeout=5000)
        await cookie_btn.click()
    except:
        pass
    postcode_input = await page.wait_for_selector("//input[@autocomplete='postal-code']")
    await postcode_input.fill(postcode)
    await postcode_input.press('Enter')
    address_select = await page.wait_for_selector('//select')
    await address_select.select_option(value=uprn)
    next_btn = await page.wait_for_selector("//button[contains(text(), 'Next')]")
    await next_btn.click()
    await page.wait_for_selector("//h1[contains(text(), 'When is my bin collection date?')]")
    table = await page.wait_for_selector("//h1[contains(text(), 'When is my bin collection date?')]/following::table[1]")
    return await table.inner_html()
data.notes: Handles Cloudflare challenges with wait times and requires both postcode and UPRN for selection.

calendar_url = None
for row in rows:
    text = await row.inner_text()
    if house_number in text:
        import re
        match = re.search(r'(Monday|Tuesday|Wednesday|Thursday|Friday)\s+([AB])', text, re.IGNORECASE)

            day = match.group(1).lower()
            letter = match.group(2).lower()
            calendar_url = f'https://www.chelmsford.gov.uk/bins-and-recycling/check-your-collection-day/{day}-{letter}-collection-calendar/'
            break
if calendar_url:

    ics_url = await ics_link.get_attribute('href')

data.playwright_steps: Open the collection page, click cookies, expand the postcode search, enter postcode, select the address from the dropdown by matching the house number (paon), and extract collection details from the results section.
data.playwright_code:
await page.goto('https://www.eastriding.gov.uk/environment/bins-rubbish-recycling/bins-and-collections/bin-collection-dates/#')
try:
    await page.click('#er-cookie-placeholder-settings div a:has-text("Accept all")', timeout=5000)
except:
    pass
await page.click('//a[contains(text(), "Open all")]')
await page.fill("input[placeholder='Enter your postcode']", postcode)
await page.press("input[placeholder='Enter your postcode']", 'Enter')
await page.wait_for_selector("select.dropdown")
await page.select_option("select.dropdown", label=house_number)
await page.wait_for_selector(".results")
soup = BeautifulSoup(await page.content(), 'html.parser')
bin_types = {}
header_elements = soup.find_all('li', class_=lambda x: x and x.startswith('header'))
for header_element in header_elements:
    bin_type = header_element.get_text(strip=True)
    bin_class = [cls for cls in header_element.get('class') if cls != 'header']
    if bin_class:
        bin_types[bin_class[0]] = bin_type
date_elements = soup.find_all('li', class_=lambda x: x and x.startswith('date'))
for date_element in date_elements:
    bin_class = [cls for cls in date_element.get('class') if cls != 'date']
    if bin_class:
        bin_type = bin_types.get(bin_class[0])
        span_text = date_element.find('span').get_text(strip=True)
        results.append({"type": bin_type, "collectionDate": span_text})
data.notes: Uses a combination of CSS classes (header and date) to map bin types to their respective dates in the results list.

3. Click the search button.
4. Wait for the address dropdown and select the address matching the house number.
5. Wait for the results to load.
6. Extract the bin collection text and parse the types and dates.
data.playwright_code:
async def get_bins(page, postcode, house_number):
    await page.goto('https://forms.north-norfolk.gov.uk/outreach/BinCollectionDays.ofml')
    await page.fill('#F_Address_subform\\:Postcode', postcode)
    await page.click('#BA_Address_subform\\:Search_button')
    await page.wait_for_selector('#F_Address_subform\\:Id')
    
    # Select the address containing the house number
    options = await page.query_selector_all('#F_Address_subform\\:Id option')
    for option in options:
        text = await option.inner_text()
        if house_number.lower() in text.lower():
            await option.click()
            break
    
    await page.wait_for_selector('div#Search_result_details_cps_hd')
    content = await page.content()
    return content

data.api_description: Navigates to a PHP endpoint that returns JSON data within the body tag after bypassing potential Cloudflare checks.
data.playwright_steps:
1. Navigate to the API endpoint with the UPRN in the query string.
2. Wait for the page load and any anti-bot checks to complete.
3. Extract the text content from the <body> tag.
4. Parse the text content as JSON.
5. Iterate through the JSON objects to extract 'type' and 'nextDate'.
data.playwright_code:
async def get_bins(uprn):
    url = f'https://www.thanet.gov.uk/wp-content/mu-plugins/collection-day/incl/mu-collection-day-calls.php?pAddress={uprn}'
    await page.goto(url)
    await page.wait_for_load_state('networkidle')
    body_content = await page.inner_text('body')
    import json
    bin_data = json.loads(body_content)
    results = []
    for item in bin_data:
        results.append({
            'type': item['type'],
            'collectionDate': item['nextDate'].split(' ')[0]
        })
    return results
data.notes: The site uses a PHP endpoint that outputs raw JSON into the HTML body. It is often protected by Cloudflare, necessitating a browser-based approach rather than a direct GET request.

data.playwright_steps: Navigate to the Wokingham waste collection page. Enter the postcode into the search box. Select the address from the dropdown that matches the provided PAON. Click the button to show collection dates. Extract the waste type and date from the resulting card elements.
data.playwright_code:
async def scrape_bins(page, postcode, paon):
    await page.goto('https://www.wokingham.gov.uk/rubbish-and-recycling/waste-collection/find-your-bin-collection-day')
    await page.wait_for_selector('#edit-postcode-search-csv')
    await page.fill('#edit-postcode-search-csv', postcode)
    await page.keyboard.press('Enter')
    
    # Select address using PAON
    await page.wait_for_selector('#edit-address-options-csv')
    option_xpath = f"//*[@id='edit-address-options-csv']//option[starts-with(normalize-space(.), '{paon}')]"
    await page.click(option_xpath)
    
    # Submit the form to show dates
    await page.click('#edit-show-collection-dates-csv')
    
    # Wait for results to load
    await page.wait_for_selector('.card.card--waste.card--blue-light')
    
    cards = await page.query_selector_all('.card.card--waste.card--blue-light')
    bins = []
    for card in cards:
        type_el = await card.query_selector('h3.heading--sub')
        date_el = await card.query_selector('.card__date')
        if type_el and date_el:
            waste_type = (await type_el.inner_text()).split('(')[0].strip()
            raw_date = (await date_el.inner_text()).split(' ')[1].strip()
            bins.append({
                "type": waste_type,
                "collectionDate": raw_date
            })
    return bins
data.notes: Uses PAON for address matching within a dropdown list generated after postcode submission.

data.notes: The site uses a standard form-based lookup that requires a UPRN to select the specific address from the dropdown.

data.api_description: Calls the Wyre Council bin collection API using the UPRN and parses the HTML response returned in the JSON payload.
data.notes: The API returns HTML. Bin types are extracted from the h3 heading and dates from the div with class bin-collection-tasks__content. Ordinal indicators need to be removed before parsing.

data.calendar_description: The council uses a bi-weekly alternating schedule. Black/Grey bins and Green recycling bins are collected every 14 days on an offset schedule based on the day of the week associated with the property.
data.notes: The collection day (paon input) is used to calculate the offset from the base start dates (Refuse: 2024-11-25, Recycling: 2024-11-18). Collections occur every 14 days.

data.api_description: Calls the York waste API with a UPRN to retrieve bin collection services and dates.
data.notes: The API returns a JSON object with a 'services' array containing 'service' and 'nextCollection' fields.
